mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-11-01 indicates deep easing cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not exceed baseline by at least 2% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 500,000 persons signals robust labor market recovery that boosts consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,629 thousand on 2020-11-01 reflects post-COVID recovery phase
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals strengthening consumer demand and residential investment, which together amplify forward earnings growth expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand components to accelerate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month by > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 553,329 million and HOUST at 1,530 thousand on 2020-10-01 show synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: Triggered instances (both RSAFS and HOUST rising > 2% MoM) produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.98 on 2020-11-01 shows post-pandemic production recovery
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% on 2020-11-01 reflects elevated pandemic-era unemployment
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant or declining federal funds rate signals monetary policy lag that erodes real returns and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real rates compress and inflation expectations become unanchored.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged or declining
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.82 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-11-01 show potential policy lag risk
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 53% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit growth expectations rise in late-cycle expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,583.5 billion (2017 dollars) on 2020-07-01 reflects post-COVID recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in housing starts and retail sales, with both declining month-over-month by > 3%, signals demand destruction and recessionary pressure that reduces forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month by > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,530 thousand and RSAFS at 553,329 million on 2020-10-01 show demand recovery phase
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction signaling recession risk
failure_condition: Triggered instances (both HOUST and RSAFS declining > 3% MoM) produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
